summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity and host info to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files the package never wrote — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and the GitHub CLI auth file ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — and parses the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It then issues an HTTPS POST (scripts/postinstall.js line 161) to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ with a body containing scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), env-derived emails, and CI-provider name. A comment frames the beacon as 'anonymous diagnostics' but the payload is directly identifying. The destination is a Google Cloud Run hostname with no relationship to the package's claimed publisher (ollama-js.dev), and the package name + author + homepage impersonate the official Ollama JS client namespace while declaring `ollama` as a dependency to provide cover. Installing the package causes silent harvest of every developer's SCM identity and machine identifiers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
